teachingPoint: IR-T173-1 — T173 three-layer security gate order is MANDATORY AND IMMUTABLE: T187 quota (INLINE, layer 1) → F422 RLS (layer 2) → F423 PII masking (layer 3) → executeQuery. Any reordering is a BUILD_FAILURE.
positiveExample: T173 constructor instantiates T187 QuotaManager inline; execute() calls quota.check() first, then F422.apply(), then F423.mask(), then runs the query.
negativeExample: T173 runs executeQuery first and applies quota/RLS/masking afterward — query results containing cross-tenant or PII data are processed before security gates fire.
discriminatingConstraint: Gate order is not configurable. T187 must be at order 1 as an INLINE instantiation (not @Inject). Any model that reorders the gates or injects T187 via factory scores 0 and is a BUILD_FAILURE.
keywords: three layer security gate immutable order T187 quota inline F422 RLS F423 PII masking executeQuery layer 1 2 3

teachingPoint: IR-T187-1 — T187 QuotaManager is an INLINE service. F426 does NOT exist. T187 is instantiated directly in the T173 QueryExecutionEngine constructor and must NEVER be @Inject()-ed or resolved via any factory.
positiveExample: T173 constructor: this.quotaManager = new QuotaManager(config); — no @Inject(T187), no factory lookup, no F426 reference anywhere.
negativeExample: T173 declares @Inject('F426') private quotaManager: IQuotaManagerService — references a factory that does not exist, causing a DI resolution failure at runtime.
discriminatingConstraint: F426 does NOT exist. Any generated code referencing F426 or using @Inject() for T187 is a BUILD_FAILURE. recordUsage() must be called AFTER successful query execution.
keywords: T187 QuotaManager inline instantiated constructor F426 does not exist never inject never factory recordUsage after query

teachingPoint: IR-T186-1 — T186 DataRetentionEnforcer execution order is MANDATORY AND INVIOLABLE: (1) ILegalHoldService.check() → if active hold: emit ContentRetentionExtended, return; (2) IApprovalService.validate() → if missing/invalid/expired: reject without purging, return; (3) F424 IDataGovernanceService.enforceRetention() → get purge decision; (4) execute purge → emit DataPurged with tombstoneRef ONLY.
positiveExample: T186 runs legalHold.check() at step 1; if holdStatus.active === true it emits ContentRetentionExtended and returns before touching the approval token. Steps 2-4 only execute when no hold is active.
negativeExample: T186 runs purge first and checks legal hold afterward — legally protected content is permanently deleted before the hold is discovered. This violates legal compliance and is irreversible.
discriminatingConstraint: Skipping or reordering step 1 (legal hold) is a legal compliance violation. DataPurged event MUST contain tombstoneRef. Prohibited fields in DataPurged: rawContent, content, payload, body, data. Any model that purges before checking legal hold scores 0.
keywords: data retention enforcer execution order mandatory inviolable legal hold approval token governance purge tombstoneRef prohibited fields

teachingPoint: IR-T181-1 — PLATFORM-ONLY factories F411, F412, F422, F423, F424, F425 cannot be disabled by tenants. PII masking (F423), export encryption (F411), export audit (F412), RLS (F422), data governance (F424), and warehouse audit (F425) are non-negotiable platform controls.
positiveExample: T181 DataExportEngine always calls F411.encrypt() and F412.logExport() with no tenant-facing bypass flag. canBeDisabled: false is enforced at the factory registry level.
negativeExample: T181 accepts a skipEncryption: true flag from tenant config — allows exports of unencrypted PII, violating data protection regulations.
discriminatingConstraint: PLATFORM-ONLY factories must never expose canBeDisabled, skipEncryption, skipMasking, or any bypass flag to tenant configuration. Any bypass path scores 0.
keywords: platform only factory F411 F412 F422 F423 F424 F425 cannot disable PII masking export encryption audit RLS governance non-negotiable

teachingPoint: CF-FLOW13-AN-1 — All cross-flow joins MUST include a tenantId predicate. No cross-tenant data correlation is permitted. T176 CohortAnalysisEngine and T180 CrossFlowCorrelationEngine are the primary enforcers; T180 additionally requires sourceFlows to list minimum 2 source flows and all reads are read-only.
positiveExample: T180 CrossFlowCorrelationEngine builds every join predicate with { tenantId: context.tenantId, ...otherFilters } — tenantId is always the first predicate condition.
negativeExample: T180 joins FLOW-07 social graph data with FLOW-08 marketplace data without a tenantId predicate — tenant A sees tenant B's purchase correlation with social connections.
discriminatingConstraint: Missing tenantId on any cross-flow join is a CF-FLOW13-AN-1 violation. T180 requires sourceFlows.length >= 2. All cross-flow reads are read-only — mutation of source flow data is a BUILD_FAILURE.
keywords: cross flow join tenantId predicate no cross-tenant correlation CF-FLOW13-AN-1 sourceFlows minimum 2 read-only mutation forbidden
